should_trigger: Design an auto-orchestrator policy that assigns coding tasks to three subagents under token budgets, API rate limits, deadlines, and different expected success rates.
We need to choose which tools and retry branches to run so total cost stays under $20 while maximizing probability of completing the user request before the SLA.
Model our agent queue: requests arrive faster during business hours, reviewers are limited, and we need thresholds for when to spawn extra workers or defer low-priority work.
Turn this workflow into variables, constraints, and an objective so we can optimize latency versus quality without violating approval gates.
Audit this orchestrator for hidden bottlenecks, binding constraints, and sensitivity to a lower API quota.
should_not_trigger: Break this feature request into a work breakdown structure with deliverables and milestones, but no resource optimization.
Explain what Operations Research means in one paragraph for a glossary.
Write a simple sequential runbook for deploying a static website; there are no alternative assignments, capacity limits, or tradeoffs.
edge_cases: There are only two possible tools and one is usually better. Decide whether a simple rule is enough or whether an OR model is justified by cost, risk, quota, or deadline constraints.
The user wants maximum quality, minimum cost, and fastest completion but has not said how to trade them off. Surface the missing objective policy before optimizing.
We have rough failure-rate estimates from past runs. Decide whether to use deterministic allocation, scenario analysis, or simulation.

compliance_expectations.should_trigger: Activate the skill, apply the execution gate, define the decision frame, variables, parameters, objective policy, constraints, model family, solve approach, policy translation, monitoring, and sensitivity checks without opening or requiring external files, webpages, original sources, source reports, external source folders, or source snapshots.
compliance_expectations.should_not_trigger: Do not activate for pure WBS, glossary, or linear runbook requests with no competing choices or scarce constraints.
compliance_expectations.edge_cases: Clarify missing tradeoff policy before optimizing; choose a simple rule, deterministic model, scenario analysis, or simulation based on measured stakes and uncertainty.
